name: Gig Life
white: A $9 delivery fee on $6 of food.
Tipping the self-checkout machine out of fear.
Five stars, given under duress.
Surge pricing during the hurricane.
My driver, who is making seven other stops with my ice cream.
A photo of my food on a porch that is not my porch.
Renting out my own bed as an "experience."
The app's 40% cut.
Being my own boss, who is an app.
"Your order is 3 minutes away" for 45 minutes.
A grown man sprinting through a hospital lobby with one milkshake.
The delivery guy eating exactly three of my fries.
A tip screen with options for 25%, 30%, and "coward."
The scooter graveyard at the bottom of the river.
Gig-economy health insurance, which is a coupon.
My driver's check-engine light, which we are both ignoring.
A "contactless" delivery with a lot of eye contact.
Rating a human being out of five stars while he watches.
My passenger rating, which is somehow a 3.1.
The driver who circled my block eleven times and then cancelled.
A ghost kitchen that is four restaurants and one microwave.
Surge pricing on insulin.
A helicopter delivering one Diet Coke.
My driver's "I'm basically an entrepreneur" monologue.
The bathroom I am legally not allowed to use.
Peeing in a bottle to protect my acceptance rate.
A "delivered" photo of a bush.
Tipping in the app, tipping in cash, and then apologizing.
Forty dollars of cold pad thai.
A rideshare pool with a man eating soup.
The passenger who wants to discuss crypto for forty minutes.
A dashboard holding two phones, a tablet, and a rosary.
The "hot bag," which is neither.
Room-temperature sushi, hand-delivered by a stranger.
A thumbprint in my ramen, included free.
The app asking me how the app is doing.
My driver's 4.97 rating and dead eyes.
The "flexible schedule" of 4 a.m. to 4 a.m.
A side hustle for my side hustle.
My fourth job, which is also an app.
A retirement plan that is just the word "hustle."
The phrase "independent contractor," whispered like a curse.
A $190 cleaning fee on a $60 air mattress.
A chore list longer than my lease.
The host who "lives in the basement, don't worry about it."
The smoke detector that is also, per the reviews, a camera.
The airport pickup zone, a lawless hellscape.
A trunk containing forty other people's burritos.
My burrito, handed between three strangers like a relay baton.
The little map car doing donuts in a parking lot.
Watching the little car icon drive away from my house.
A single lime, substituted for my birthday cake.
A candle, because they were out of eggs.
Tipping 20% on the fees.
Same-day delivery of a single AA battery.
Assembling a stranger's sex swing for $40 and a bottle of water.
A stranger doing my laundry and judging me, for $30.
Delivering mozzarella sticks to an orgy.
My driver asking if I "party."
Emergency condoms, delivered twenty minutes too late.
Being asked to rate my hookup out of five stars.
A bottle of what I hope is apple juice in the door pocket.
Surge pricing at 2:01 a.m. outside the club.
A $70 ride home from a $12 night out.
A war podcast at volume ten.
The pin drop that put me in a lake.
A gate code that doesn't work and a customer who doesn't answer.
Leaving it with "the doorman," who is a raccoon.
The porch pirate who left a five-star review.
Free delivery, plus a $12 fee for the free delivery.
A subscription to avoid the fees invented to sell the subscription.
A confetti animation instead of a raise.
"Diamond Driver status," which comes with nothing.
A 34-stop "optimized" route.
Delivering a pizza to another, better pizza place.
The training video where a cartoon car calls me family.
Getting fired by an email with no reply address.
A support chatbot trained exclusively on the word "Unfortunately."
The $0 tip with a "God bless."
A minivan with 340,000 miles and a dream.

My rideshare driver spent the entire trip telling me about _.
The app's new premium tier unlocks _.
I knew surge pricing had gone too far when a ride home cost me _.
My five-star rating did not survive _.
The startup's entire pitch: _, but on demand.
In lieu of a tip, the customer left me _.
The delivery confirmation photo clearly shows _.
I found _ in the back seat of my rideshare.
Instead of health insurance, the app gave us all _.
The listing said "cozy" and "charming." It did not mention _.
The algorithm deactivated my account overnight, citing _.
My passenger asked if we could make "one quick stop" for _.
The self-checkout machine now asks for a tip, your zip code, and _.
Welcome to the gig economy, where your boss is an app and your severance is _.
They were out of what I ordered, so my shopper substituted _.
According to the tracking map, my driver is currently at _.
The order was simple: _. What showed up 90 minutes later: _.
To make rent this month, I'm renting out _ and delivering _.
